skill.name: kpi-dashboard-design
skill.description: Design KPI dashboards with clear metric selection, practical layout patterns, visualization guidance, and sample implementation approaches for business reporting.
skill.summary: Plan business KPI dashboards that connect metrics, layouts, alerts, and governance.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, sensitive data, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, network, and composite dangerous-pattern findings. Manual review found no executable shell behavior, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent; the hits are Markdown examples, dashboard terminology, SQL examples, and ordinary resource links. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from external documentation links.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives. The cited lines are Markdown code fence delimiters around SMART criteria, dashboard sketches, YAML lists, SQL examples, and a Streamlit example; they are not executable instructions and do not interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present only as Markdown fence syntax. The surrounding content is static documentation and sample dashboard material, with no shell command or runtime execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dashboard and SQL Terms Flagged as Sensitive or Reconnaissance Behavior
low_findings.description: Static findings for Windows SAM database access, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance are false positives. The cited lines contain the skill name, dashboard prose, a system health label, SQL joins, and dashboard best practices, not credential files, cryptographic routines, or host discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no references to credential stores, cryptographic algorithms, or host reconnaissance commands. The matching tokens appear in business and dashboard examples.

low_findings.title: Benign External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded links in a Resources section. They point to dashboard design references and do not instruct the model to fetch URLs, transmit data, or call an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are ordinary Markdown reference links and are isolated from any executable code. No evidence of network automation or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Composite Dangerous Combination Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic for code execution, network activity, and credential access is not confirmed. Manual review found no real code execution, credential access, prompt injection, obfuscation, or exfiltration behavior in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The composite finding depends on other scanner hits that were individually false positives. The file is a single Markdown document containing guidance and examples.

content.user_title: Design KPI Dashboards That Drive Decisions
content.value_statement: Teams often build dashboards that show many numbers but do not guide action. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code select meaningful KPIs, structure views, and present metrics clearly.
actual_capabilities: Defines strategic, tactical, and operational KPI dashboard patterns.
Recommends common KPIs for sales, marketing, product, and finance teams.
Provides dashboard layout patterns for executives, SaaS metrics, and operations monitoring.
Includes sample SQL approaches for MRR, cohort retention, and CAC calculations.
Shows a Streamlit dashboard example using pandas and Plotly.
Lists practical dashboard design dos and do nots.
limitations: Does not connect to live databases or analytics platforms.
Does not validate metric definitions against a specific company data model.
Provides example calculations that may need adaptation for local schemas.
Does not include production deployment, authentication, or access control guidance.

use_cases.title: Create an Executive Metrics View
use_cases.description: Design a concise dashboard with headline KPIs, trend indicators, and alerts for leadership review.

use_cases.title: Select Department KPIs
use_cases.description: Choose focused sales, marketing, product, or finance metrics that match each team's operating goals.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Data Dashboard
use_cases.description: Use the sample SQL and Streamlit patterns as a starting point for a reporting prototype.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Core KPIs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose 5 to 7 KPIs for a dashboard about [business area]. Include each metric's purpose, audience, update frequency, and target.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Dashboard Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a KPI dashboard layout for [audience]. Include headline metrics, supporting charts, alerts, filters, and drilldown sections.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this dashboard plan: [paste summary]. Identify weak metrics, overcrowded areas, missing context, and changes that improve decision making.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Metric Governance Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a KPI governance plan for [organization]. Define metric ownership, calculation rules, refresh cadence, quality checks, and change control.

output_examples.output: A one-page layout with MRR, ARR, churn, retention, CAC payback, revenue trend, cohort retention, and exception alerts.

output_examples.input: Pick marketing KPIs for a monthly review.
output_examples.output: A focused metric set covering acquisition cost, lead volume, conversion rate, channel performance, campaign ROI, and CAC payback.
A real-time view with service health, throughput, error rate, latency alerts, and recent incident status.

best_practices: Start with business decisions, then choose the smallest useful KPI set.
Show context through targets, trends, comparisons, and alert thresholds.
Document metric definitions so teams interpret results consistently.
anti_patterns: Do not fill dashboards with vanity metrics that do not guide action.
Do not mix strategic, tactical, and operational audiences in one crowded view.
Do not use visual effects that make values harder to compare.

faq.question: Can this skill build a complete dashboard application?
faq.answer: It provides design guidance and example implementation patterns. Production integration, security, and deployment still require engineering work.

faq.question: Which teams does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers sales, marketing, product, finance, executive reporting, SaaS metrics, and real-time operations monitoring.

faq.question: Does it include SQL examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes sample approaches for monthly recurring revenue, cohort retention, and customer acquisition cost.

faq.question: Does it support real-time monitoring design?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an operations center pattern with service health, throughput, error rate, and recent alerts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The content is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for dashboard planning and prototype guidance.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare the target audience, business goals, available data sources, metric definitions, and refresh requirements.
